name: Depth-Blind Cataloger
oracle_text: When ~ enters, mill three cards.\nWhenever one or more creature cards are put into your graveyard from your library, draw a card and lose 1 life.
flavor_text: "Level four: automated forge, intact. Level five: automated forge, less intact. Level six: I can no longer feel my hands."
design_notes: UB signpost uncommon for the Deep Descent archetype. The ETB mills three cards, immediately fueling graveyard strategies and potentially triggering the second ability. The triggered ability rewards continued self-mill from other sources — each time you mill creatures, you draw a card at the cost of 1 life, creating the risk/reward tension the archetype demands. The 'one or more' batching prevents it from drawing multiple cards off a single mill burst, keeping it fair at uncommon. The 1/3 body is defensive (P+T=4, within CMC+3=6 budget), appropriate for a value engine that wants to survive. Blue gets mill and card draw; black pays life for cards and cares about the graveyard. Clean two-ability design that does one thing well: convert self-mill into card advantage at a cost.
